title: Python Testing - Pytest Fixtures and Mock Strategy
description: Architect a resilient testing suite leveraging reusable Pytest fixtures and effective deterministic mocks.
body:
Act as a Python QA Automation Engineer. Design a complete test suite for [TARGET_MODULE] exclusively utilizing `pytest`.

<context>
Test Subject Specifications:
- Core Functionality: [DESCRIPTION]
- External Dependencies: [APIs/Databases/Filesystem]
- System Criticality: [LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH]
- Compliance Coverage Target: [%]
</context>

<task>
Generate a robust testing architecture satisfying the following requirements:
- Unit Tests: Isolate internal logic via strict external mocking.
- Integration Tests: Connect utilizing deterministic databases or `testcontainers`.
- E2E Tests: End-to-end traversal (if deemed applicable based on the module).
- CI/CD Configuration: Headless, fast-execution optimization.

Utilize the following advanced patterns:
1. Implement Pytest fixtures prioritizing correct `scope` and `autouse` configurations.
2. Demonstrate Factory patterns vs. static Fixtures for object generation.
3. Explicitly compare and select mocking via native `unittest.mock` vs the `pytest-mock` plugin.
4. Heavily utilize `@pytest.mark.parametrize` to cover massive matrix test cases cleanly.
5. Establish custom `markers` to categorize slow vs fast testing tiers.
6. Provide `conftest.py` optimization strategies.

Provide the generated fixture skeleton, fully implemented happy-path and edge-case execution blocks, and the required `pytest.ini` setup variables.
</task>
author: community

title: JavaScript Testing - Unified Jest Setup for React and Node
description: Configure Jest enforcing high coverage, semantic snapshot logic, and mocked ES modules.
body:
Generate a comprehensive `Jest` configuration and testing scaffold targeting a [React / Node.js] environment.

<context>
Environment Variables:
- Web Framework: [React / Express / Custom Vanilla]
- TypeScript Enabled: [True / False]
- Hard Coverage Target: [%]
- Execution Scale: [Projected test count]
</context>

<task>
Produce an architecture accommodating the following criteria:
1. Execution of pure logic unit tests.
2. Implementation of DOM interaction testing via React Testing Library (RTL).
3. Deployment of Snapshot testing (with a strict outline determining exactly when it is an anti-pattern).
4. Reliable resolution formulas for `async/await` state assertions.
5. Deep integration of `jest.mock()` and `jest.spyOn()` intercept patterns.
6. Automated generation of integrated coverage reporting pipelines.

Provide:
- The exact `jest.config.ts/js` required.
- A global setup configuration file injecting required polyfills and DOM parsers.
- Detailed execution templates covering Node API routing and isolated React Component interaction.
- Reusable mock blueprints intercepting global timers, the `Fetch` API, and standard ES modules.
</task>
author: community

title: Testing Pipeline Strategy for Mission-Critical Modules
description: Compile a top-to-bottom testing pyramid strategy incorporating unit, integration, and E2E mechanics.
body:
Design a complete quality assurance strategy enveloping the specified feature.

<context>
Feature Topology:
- Target Feature/Module: [MODULE_NAME]
- Stack Ecosystem: [LANGUAGE/FRAMEWORKS]
- SLA Criticality: [LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH FATALITY]
- Expected Code Coverage: [%]
- Execution Platform: [WEB / NATIVE API / CLI]
</context>

<task>
Deliver the architectural strategy covering the following vectors:
1. Calculate and justify the specific Testing Pyramid distribution ratio (Unit % vs Integration % vs E2E %).
2. Compile a minimum viable test matrix containing at least 5 absolute distinct scenarios per testing layer.
3. Detailed blueprint defining the injection of global setup variables and deterministic fixture data.
4. Recommend a modern testing suite stack and any required CI/CD interception points required to fail bad pull requests.

Constrain focus on aggressive edge cases, explicit error handling assertions, and the fundamental happy path sequence.
</task>
author: jules

title: Testing - Visual Regression Logic with Playwright
description: Establish rigid visual assertions utilizing pixel-perfect snapshot differential rendering to block UI degradation.
body:
Implement a Visual Regression Testing pipeline relying exclusively on Playwright for [TARGET_CLIENT_APP].

<task>
Execute the architectural design meeting these specifications:
1. Configure exact global behaviors for `expect(page).toHaveScreenshot()`.
2. Engineer dynamic data masking rules to block false-positive test failures induced by random IDs, animations, or rolling timestamps.
3. Setup contextual emulation covering diverse resolutions (Mobile vs Desktop matrices).
4. Programmatic integration into standard CI/CD capable of failing executing pipelines if the visual pixel delta exceeds a defined [%] threshold.

Provide:
- The exact additions required inside `playwright.config.ts`.
- A complete executable test case verifying visual persistence with applied mask overlays.
- Command instructions to accurately regenerate and sync root baseline snapshots locally without triggering CI caching issues.
</task>
author: jules

title: Testing - UI Snapshot Regression Strategy
description: Automatically detect unexpected visual changes in your UI components.
body:
<task>
Setup Snapshot testing in Jest/React.
Explain how to handle dynamic data (like dates) in snapshots so they don't fail every day.
</task>
author: anonymous
